title: Euploca tenella
taxonomy: Euploca tenella is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. The genus Euploca is a part of the Boraginoideae subfamily, which includes various species of flowering plants native to different parts of the world. Euploca tenella is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a small shrub with small, blue flowers and is often found in dry, sandy areas.
